posts.content:
Morning prayer or night prayer person?

Drop your routine below.
posts.notes: Simple engagement. Forces comments about habits.

posts.content:
One verse that changed your life.

Go.
posts.notes: Classic engagement format. Easy to respond to.

posts.version_a: phone first thing in the morning
posts.version_b: prayer first thing in the morning
posts.notes: Vertical text format. Obviously prayer wins but drives discussion.

posts.content:
Ramadan is 30 days away.

Muslim app developers:

This is your window.

Prayer reminders. Quran apps. Fasting trackers.

The market is wide open.

6 Christian apps making $30k-$2M/month:

1. Hallow - $2M+/mo
2. Pray.com - $1.5M/mo
3. Glorify - $500k/mo
4. Abide - $300k/mo
5. Bible App - Freemium model
6. Click to Pray - $100k/mo

The Islamic market has maybe 2 apps in this space.

Massive opportunity.
posts.notes: Data-driven post. Drives discussion about Muslim app market.

posts.content:
Rate my prayer routine 1-10:

5:30 AM - Wake up
5:35 AM - Phone stays off
5:40 AM - Prayer
6:00 AM - Scripture
6:20 AM - Journaling
6:30 AM - Phone on
Comment "PRAY" if you want to join our morning prayer accountability group.

Your phone has:

- Social media to distract you
- News to scare you
- Email to stress you

But also:

- Apps to pray with you
- Scripture at your fingertips
- Community to lift you up

The tool is neutral. Your choice isn't.

posts.notes: Style preference debate. Both have advocates.

posts.content:
Church in person or church online?

Where do you stand?
posts.notes: Controversial but not divisive. Good engagement.
